Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Top grid has 3 N objects remaining (cols 25-28, 31-34, 43-46) and 1 N in bottom grid (col 43-46). - After clicking an N, a G frame appears around it and a hollow square appears to its left. - Clicking the hollow square removes all Ns up to that point. - Goal: remove all N objects from the grids.
- Plan: Click the hollow square that appeared after selecting the N at col 31-34. This should remove the Ns at cols 25-28 and 31-34.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the ascii output, I can see the N objects:
- Top grid: N at cols 37-40, N at cols 43-46 (rows 15-18)
- Bottom grid: N at cols 43-46 (rows 51-54)
The N at 37-40 in the top grid didn't respond when I clicked on it. Let me look at the bottom grid area more carefully. The bottom grid has N at cols 43-46, rows 51-54.
Looking at the ascii:
Row 51: `...BWwNNwWWwwwwWBb...`
Row 52: `...BNNNNWWwwwwWBb...`
Row 53: `...BNNNNWWwwwwWBb...`  
Row 54: `...BWwNNwWWwwwwWBb...`
So the N in the bottom grid is at rows 51-54, cols 43-46. The center would be around row 52-53, col 44-45.
Wait, I need to reconsider the game mechanics. Let me look at what happened in the previous turns. The history shows:
- Step 28: N objects at cols 25-28, 31-34, 43-46 in top grid, and one in bottom grid
- Step 29: After clicking on N at 31-34, a G frame appeared around it and a hollow square appeared to its left
- Step 30: After clicking the hollow square, the Ns at 25-28 and 31-34 were removed, and a new N appeared at 37-40
So the pattern seems to be:
1. Click on an N to select it (G frame appears)
2. Click on the hollow square that appears to the left
3. This removes Ns up to that point and spawns a new N
But when I clicked on the N at 37-40, nothing happened. This is strange. Let me check if maybe the click position was wrong or if there's some other issue.
Actually, looking at the ascii output, the N at 37-40 is at:
Row 15: `...WWwNNwWWwNNwW...`
Row 16: `...WWNNNNWWNNNNW...`
Row 17: `...WWNNNNWWNNNNW...`
Row 18: `...WWwNNwWWwNNwW...`
The N cells are at positions where the character is 'N'. For the N at cols 37-40:
- Row 15: cols 38-39 are 'N' (w at 37, 40)
- Row 16: cols 37-40 are 'N'
- Row 17: cols 37-40 are 'N'
- Row 18: cols 38-39 are 'N' (w at 37, 40)
So the center of the N object would be around row 16-17, col 38-39. I clicked at row 16, col 38, which should be within the N object.
Hmm, but nothing happened. Let me try clicking on the N at 43-46 in the top grid instead. The center would be around row 16-17, col 44-45.
Actually wait - maybe I need to reconsider the game mechanics entirely. Let me look at the history more carefully.
Looking at the step images:
- Step 28: Shows N objects at different positions
- Step 29: Shows changes after clicking
- Step 30: Shows further changes
- Step 31: Shows the current state
The fact that clicking on the N at 37-40 did nothing suggests that maybe the game mechanic has changed, or I need to click on a different N.
Let me try clicking on the N at 43-46 in the top grid. The center would be around row 16, col 44.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking N at 37-40 did nothing. Let me try clicking on N at 43-46 in the top grid instead
# The N at 43-46 is at rows 15-18, cols 43-46
# Center would be around row 16-17, col 44-45
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 44}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
